text: Company Policies Master Document

BENEFITS
EMPLOYEE BENEFITS POLICY — HR Department
- Comprehensive health insurance covering employee, spouse, and up to 2 children.
- Coverage amount: Up to $500,000 per year.
- Includes hospitalization, outpatient care, dental, and vision.
- Pre-existing conditions covered after 1 year of continuous employment.
2. Retirement Benefits
- 401(k) plan with company match up to 6% of base salary.
- Vesting schedule: 25% per year, fully vested after 4 years.
- Employees can adjust contribution percentage at any time.
3. Professional Development
- Annual learning budget of $2,000 per employee.
- Covers courses, certifications, conferences, and books.
- Must be pre-approved by the reporting manager.
- Reimbursement within 30 days of submitting receipts.
- Gym membership reimbursement up to $100/month.
- Mental health support: 6 free counseling sessions per year.
- Annual health check-up fully covered.
- Wellness days: 2 additional days off per year for mental health.
5. Employee Assistance Program (EAP)
- 24/7 confidential counseling hotline.
- Financial planning assistance.
- Legal consultation (up to 3 free sessions per year).
- Public transit subsidies up to $150/month.
- Parking allowance for office commuters: $100/month.
- Bicycle purchase assistance program.
- Free meals at the office cafeteria.
- Company-sponsored team outings (quarterly).
- Employee referral bonus: $2,000 per successful hire.
- Birthday and work anniversary gifts.

LEAVE-POLICY
LEAVE POLICY — HR Department
- All full-time employees are entitled to 20 days of paid annual leave per calendar year.
- Leave accrues at a rate of 1.67 days per month of service.
- Unused leave can be carried forward up to a maximum of 5 days to the next year.
- Leave beyond the carry-forward limit will be forfeited on December 31st.
- Employees are entitled to 12 days of paid sick leave per year.
- A medical certificate is required for absences exceeding 3 consecutive days.
- Unused sick leave cannot be carried forward.
- Maternity leave: 26 weeks of paid leave for birth mothers.
- Paternity leave: 2 weeks of paid leave.
- Adoption leave: 12 weeks of paid leave.
4. Leave Application Process
- Submit leave requests at least 5 business days in advance for planned leave.

- Emergency leave must be communicated to the manager within 2 hours of the start 
of the workday.
- Approval is at the discretion of the reporting manager, considering team workload.
5. Leave Without Pay (LWP)
- Available when all paid leave is exhausted.
- Requires approval from both the reporting manager and HR.
- LWP exceeding 30 days may affect employment benefits.

WORK-FROM-HOME
WORK FROM HOME POLICY — HR Department
- All employees who have completed their probation period (6 months) are eligible.

- Roles requiring physical presence (e.g., facilities, hardware) may have limited WFH 
options.
- Employees may work from home up to 3 days per week.
- At least 2 days per week must be spent in the office for collaboration.
- WFH days must be pre-approved by the reporting manager on a weekly basis.
3. Core Working Hours
- Employees must be available during core hours: 10:00 AM to 4:00 PM (local time).
- Flexibility is allowed outside core hours, provided daily work hours are met (8 hours).
4. Equipment and Setup
- The company provides a laptop and essential peripherals for WFH use.
- Employees must ensure a stable internet connection (minimum 10 Mbps).
- A dedicated workspace is recommended but not mandatory.
- Employees must be reachable via Slack, email, and video calls during core hours.
- Daily standup meetings must be attended via video with the camera on.
- Response time for messages should not exceed 30 minutes during core hours.
- Company VPN must be used when accessing internal systems.
- Confidential documents must not be printed or shared on personal devices.
- Report any security incidents to IT within 1 hour.
- WFH privileges may be revoked if productivity drops or policies are violated.
- HR will provide a written notice before revoking WFH privileges.

Z-EXTREME-ENVIRONMENTS-POLICY
Intergalactic Travel and Extreme Environments Policy

1. General Purpose
This policy outlines the rules and regulations for employees engaging in intergalactic 
travel, teleportation, or utilizing Faster-Than-Light (FTL) drives for company business. It 
also covers extreme environment assignments.

2. Teleportation Sickness Leave
Employees who experience cellular scrambling or teleportation sickness after using the 
company's quantum teleporter are entitled to up to 14 days of specialized recuperation 
leave. During this time, employees must not operate heavy machinery, pilot spacecraft, 
or engage in temporal manipulations. Medical clearance from a certified quantum 
physicist is required before returning to work.
3. Faster-Than-Light (FTL) Reimbursement

The company will reimburse fuel costs for FTL jumps made for client meetings. 
However, detours through black holes or temporal wormholes for strictly personal time 
travel will not be reimbursed and will be deducted from the employee's standard 
temporal leave balance. Receipts must include the exact parsecs traveled.

4. Alien Pet Accommodations
Employees working from remote outposts in the Andromeda galaxy or similar distant 
sectors may bring up to two non-carnivorous alien lifeforms as pets to their home office. 
The pets must not emit acidic spores, possess telepathic mind-control abilities, or 
interfere with standard Wi-Fi or subspace communication frequencies.

5. Deep Sea Base Assignments
For employees assigned to the Mariana Trench Underwater Facility, decompression 
protocols must be strictly followed. You are entitled to an extra 5 hours of sleep per day 
to account for the pressure differentials. You must also wear your standard-issue 
pressure suit during all virtual meetings to maintain professional appearances.

6. Anti-Gravity Office Protocol
When assigned to the orbital zero-gravity office, all coffee and beverages must be 
consumed from sealed pouches. Floating liquids that damage company hardware will 
result in a deduction from your lunar stipend. Additionally, employees must tether 
themselves to their desks during major solar flare events.
